name: Walnut
summary: The nut with the most plant omega-3 (ALA) by far — studied for cholesterol, heart health, and, in its own trials, cognition.
superfoodReason: Uniquely high in the plant omega-3 alpha-linolenic acid, with heart-health evidence from large trials including PREDIMED.

benefits.claim: Improves cholesterol and heart-disease risk factors
benefits.mechanism: Walnuts are dense in polyunsaturated fat, including the plant omega-3 ALA. Replacing saturated fat with these fats lowers LDL cholesterol; in the PREDIMED trial, a Mediterranean diet supplemented with mixed nuts reduced major cardiovascular events.

citations.title: Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease with a Mediterranean diet supplemented with extra-virgin olive oil or nuts (Estruch et al., PREDIMED, N Engl J Med 2018)

benefits.claim: May support brain function and mood
benefits.mechanism: Walnuts supply ALA, polyphenols, and vitamin E, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain. Trials feeding walnuts have reported small improvements in cognitive measures and mood.

citations.title: Effects of walnut consumption on cognitive performance in young adults (Pribis et al., Br J Nutr 2012)

surprisingFacts.text: A walnut half looks strikingly like a tiny brain — and it happens to be the nut most studied for cognitive benefits, thanks to its omega-3 content.
